title: Mildly Roasted Turkish Sweetcorn Pilaf
description: A low-calorie, double-fried Turkish dish featuring sweetcorn, served in a Korean brass bowl, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sweetcorn with 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, salt, and black pepper until the corn is evenly coated.
Spread the sweetcorn mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the roasted sweetcorn, turkish spice blend, and cooked rice to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Cook the pilaf over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice is heated through and starting to brown.
Transfer the pilaf to a Korean brass bowl and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
